taskDescription: Click on the key anatomical terms related to male external genitalia in the text below.
textField:
The testis is enclosed by the *tunica* *albuginea*, a dense fibrous capsule that sends inward *septula* dividing the organ into approximately 250 lobules. Each lobule contains *seminiferous* tubules where *spermatogenesis* occurs. *Sertoli* cells support and nourish developing sperm, while *Leydig* cells in the interstitium produce *testosterone*.

The coverings of the *spermatic* cord from outside in are: external *spermatic* fascia (from external oblique), *cremasteric* muscle and fascia (from internal oblique), and internal spermatic fascia (from *transversalis* fascia). The *cremaster* reflex elevates the testis and is mediated by the *genitofemoral* nerve.

*Varicocele* occurs predominantly on the left side because the left *testicular* vein drains into the left *renal* vein at a right angle. The *gubernaculum* guides testicular descent. Lymphatic drainage of the testis is to *para-aortic* nodes (reflecting its *retroperitoneal* origin), while the *scrotum* drains to *inguinal* nodes. *Cryptorchidism* (undescended testis) increases the risk of malignancy and *infertility*.

overallFeedback.feedback: Good effort! Some key terms were missed.
Excellent! You identified the key terms.
